T2.DMM 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Getting Started with the Concept Management Model Package

    Create Unique IDs for CDM Tables

    create_unique_id.Rd

    This function generates a unique CDM ID for each data record of the specified Database CDM instance. This step is suggested to be applied after using the loadDb function. The function modifies the specified database by creating unique IDs for the specified CDM tables.

    create_unique_id(
  db_connection,
  cdm_tables_names,
  extension_name = "",
  schema_name = NULL,
  to_view = FALSE,
  pipeline_extension = "_T2DMM"
)

    Arguments

    db_connection

    Database connection object.

    cdm_tables_names

    List of CDM tables names to be imported into the db.

    extension_name

    String to be added to the name of the tables, useful when loading different CDM instances in the same database.

    schema_name

    Optional schema name to prepend to table and view names. Default is `NULL`.

    to_view

    Logical. If `TRUE` (default), creates a view with the unique ID column. If `FALSE`, overwrites the original table.

    pipeline_extension

    When using views when applying the clean_missing_values on CDM table we must define the name of the pipeline extension. See add_view for more information.

    Examples

    if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Example usage of create_unique_id
db_connection <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:")
cdm_tables_names <- c("PERSONS", "VISITS", "OBSERVATIONS")
create_unique_id(db_connection, cdm_tables_names,
  extension_name = "_CDM1"
)
} # }
